desc:
Gravel and dark-green weeds cover the old, decayed road. Dense bushes line it on
both sides, trying to conquer all the open space available. The trees along the
road grow like giant pillars, their gnarled trunks rising to great heights,
disappearing from sight into the darkness of the shadowy canopy. To the north,
the forest is more open, and a clearing can be seen through the trees.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
Some old tracks are visible on the sand of the bumpy road that runs here through
a lush grove of huge beech trees. Some of the trees are really massive, so big
that the road could run directly through the trunk and still it would be just a
hole in the bark. The enormous roots are like veins on the surface of the
ground, some of them crossing the road.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
Huge, gloomy oak trees reach over the narrow road that snakes through the
forest. Shrubs and tangled berry bushes border the road, limiting visibility to
a minimum. The surface of the road itself is dusty and sandy, and covered by dry
weeds and occasional litter that has fallen down from the engulfing forest.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
Tall trees reach high into the sky and bend their trunks and branches towards
the centre of the road. The road appears to be very old, but, very strangely,
quite well maintained. To the east and west the road continues on through the
dark and dreary forest. Along the south edge of the road a tree wall can be
seen. Fallen trees would prevent any move to the north.
name: Dark Road through the Forest

desc:
The Old Forest Road runs through a dark grove where large, twisted trees line
the immediate border of the road. One of the gigantic oaks has fallen onto the
road and is now slowly rotting away on the path. Spider webs cover the trees,
and dead branches and other forest litter lie on the ground. To the northeast,
an opening is faintly visible in the distance.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
To the north and southeast the forest has faded into the distance. Tufts of
green grass poke their way through the black and grey brush. Directly to the
south the forest remains dense and dreary. The flowing meadows here are much
easier to traverse, not least due to the absence of such dark thick trees.
Further east the forest seems to lighten as the meadow expands further south.
name: Old Forest Road along a Meadow

desc:
Meadows stretch along this section of the road, giving one a good view of the
surrounding terrain. The road here is composed of gravel and appears to be very
old. Although in some places large boulders rest on the road, overall it is
quite smooth and easy to travel on. It leads straight through the meadow from
east to west, where it again enters the forest, which can already be seen down
the path.
name: Road at the Edge of a Meadow

desc:
The forest road continues to the east and west. To the south, a large flowing
meadow can be seen where tall grasses sway back and forth in rhythmic motion,
giving this area a sense of calm. The otherwise lifeless meadow emits a foul
stench. To the north the mighty forest recedes.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
The road changes a bit here. An evil power has made the trees partly destroy the
road to the east, but it seems in a better state to the west. A giant spider web
hangs on some branches, sign of the closeness of a spider lair. To the north and
east the trees look dead, but they get gradually healthier the further to the
west one looks. However, there is not enough foliage to stop the sunshine from
reaching the ground.
name: Narrow Forest Road

desc:
The grey road sometimes almost disappears admist the trees, as though the forest
wanted to absorb it. The forest is dense, here and there a trunk has fallen
across the way, but they can easily be jumped by anyone. Lots of broken branches
lie around. To the north and south stand the sinister trees of Mirkwood, the
road continues to the east and west.
name: Road through a Dead Forest

desc:
The Old Forest Road is being ruined here by the roots which are slowly working
towards taking over the land. Many cobbles are missing, and many small plants
grow between the remaining ones. To the south, the forest seems dead, the only
living plants being small ferns. The road continues both west and east, but the
trees along it to the east seem to have more leaves.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
A clearing opens up around the road. Some missing cobbles make potholes in the
ruts that road traffic formed, but if one can judge by the plants growing
between the cobblestones, no waggon has used this road for years. The Old Forest
Road goes on to both west and east. In the west, the trees seem dead. To the
north a small hill rises above the forest.
name: Road Clearing

desc:
The road is irregular here, as some roots have grown under it, making bulges in
its surface. Some old trails lead between the trees, shortcuts across the curve
which the road makes here. The road continues to the west and to the south. To
the north, a trail snakes between tall pines. An insurmountable hedge of bushes
blocks the way to the east.
name: Irregular Forest Road

desc:
This regularly-paved road continues to the north and to the east. Two small
paths lead to the south and to the west, through Mirkwood. Trees were obviously
cut down when the road was built, hence the stumps lining the road; but the
forest came back and now trees from both sides make an arch above the road.
name: Road through Stumps

desc:
The forest is very dense and the ground sometimes seems marshy. It is quite
impossible to leave the narrow trail which snakes between the trunks. To the
west, some old, dead roots are entangled with huge bushes. To the north and
east, the Old Forest Road extends its grey shape, barely visible between the
trees.
name: Beside the Old Forest Road

desc:
The open meadow provides an uninterrupted view to the sky, a rare sight in
Mirkwood. To the north the Old Forest Road can be seen winding to the east and
west. The ground slopes to the southeast here, and gets increasingly more moist
as one moves to the west. The dark forest stands at the southern edge of the
meadow, although there seems to be a narrow gap between the trees.
name: Edge of a Wild Meadow

desc:
This corner of the meadow slopes slightly downhill, leading towards the trees in
the south. The ground gets marshier on the southern side of the meadows, and
deep animal tracks can be seen in the soft black earth. Thick black tangles of
roots and vines make their way up the slope toward the southern edge of the
flowing meadow. A young sapling sprouts in the southeast edge of the meadow, an
indication of invasion by Mirkwood from the south.
name: Wild Meadows

desc:
A grove of trees is spaced out here, along the edge of a meadow which continues
to the east. Each of the five trees has a slightly different appearance: one has
a deep, thick bark with red edges; another, a thinner shinier bark with white
accents. They stand in a rough ring shape, and their creaking in the wind makes
it seem as though they are talking to one another. To the north a road can be
seen continuing to the east and west.
name: The Foreboding Trees

desc:
This is a small open area in the forest, almost like a valley. It is surrounded
by steep, tree-covered slopes climbing in all directions but the west, where a
winding passageway runs deeper into the forest between the tree trunks and
hanging vines. One of the gigantic trees has fallen down: its crown still
strives towards the canopy, its trunk is leaning at an angle, pointing up, and
its clump of roots protrudes from the ground like an accusing arm.
name: Dead-end in the Dark Woods

desc:
Old trees have grown close to each other, their low-hanging, entwined branches
reaching downwards, as if trying to grab careless travellers. The trunks are
surrounded by black bushes and a mass of heavily-entwined, thorny shrubs that
rise from the gloomy ground. Silky spider webs have been woven all around,
blocking ways between the trees and creating sticky traps. So dense and wide is
the network of spider webs and shrubbery of vegetation that it hinders passage
in all directions save to the east.
name: Gloomy, Webbed Woods

desc:
Some huge boulders overgrown with vines and mosses block the way to the north.
To the east the boulders are replaced by trees covered in thick grey spider webs
but the end result is the same: the way is blocked. A small passageway leads to
the south and west from here, guarded by moss and lichen-covered trees which
seem to reach inwards over the path.
name: Web-covered, Gloomy Woods

desc:
In the perpetual dusk under the cover of the thick canopy the trees stand firmly
in the ground, low shrubs growing all around them and vines hanging from their
branches. To the east stands an abandoned-looking cottage. Its weather- beaten
grey walls are growing moss and mould and vines hanging from canopy have already
entwined themselves with the roof and chimney. To the south and west the forest
grows denser and no paths can be found while to the north it continues in the
same dusk that shrouds this part of the forest.
name: Gloomy Woods

desc:
Dark the surrounding trees rise, preventing the sun, the moon and the stars from
lighting up the paths. Wide the trees twist and stretch, forcing travellers to
pass only where the forest allows it. To the north the ground rises and with it
each tree seems to grasp at the opportunity to rise above its brethren, to deny
them as much space as it can. The trees on the slope are denser and more twisted
around each other than elsewhere.
name: Dark Woods

desc:
The dark trees keep up their everlasting struggle with each other for what space
still remains between them. To the south this battle has temporarily ceased: an
unusually wide and gnarled oak rules the area, with no more space left for
anything else. The forest allows travel to the west and east, and to the north
where it climbs slowly upwards, although remaining as dark and twisted as
everywhere else.
name: Dark Woods

desc:
Dark, lichen-covered trees stand tall and silent, their boughs and crowns
integrated into the seemingly endless canopy that covers Mirkwood. Great spider
webs have been woven all around, making riding impossible and forcing travellers
to move with caution to avoid getting stuck in the sticky traps. Narrow and
forever-twisting routes lead to the west and south in this inhospitable forest,
while dense vegetation blocks all other directions.
name: Dark Woods

desc:
The ground slopes upwards to form a large, tree-covered rise in the forest. The
ever-present canopy and entwined branches of the dark and gnarled trees keep the
area shadowy, providing no more visibility than elsewhere in the forest. The
slopes to the west and north are too steep and entangled to be travelled, but
one can continue on to the east or the south.
name: Rise in a Black Forest

desc:
The ground is covered in roots and strange black weeds. Old, angry-looking trees
twist around each other in their eternal struggle to reach higher and higher.
There are only narrow passageways between the pointed boughs and sharp vines
available for anyone or anything to pass deeper into the confusing forest, or to
search for an exit to less tangled parts of Mirkwood.
name: Dark Woods

desc:
Surrounding this hidden glade is a large circle of huge, twisted and bent oaks,
which leave only a small opening to the east. The oaks seem to be reaching
inwards, towards the centre of the circle, as if to claim something they want
but can not reach. The centre of the circle bears an aura of calmness and
serenity that is not present in the surrounding woods.
name: Circle of Dark Oaks

desc:
The black and dark-grey boles of the ever-bending and twisting woods close in,
shadowing the shrub-covered ground and limiting visibility. A skeleton is lying
against a huge trunk, its pale bones contrasting with the deep, dark colours of
the surrounding forest. To the south the forest opens up into a glade, while to
the north one could continue travelling deeper into the forest.
name: Dark Woods

desc:
Beech trees grow in abundance in this area, their long branches reaching upwards
and blending in to the canopy. Dense undergrowth, composed of thick ivy, shrubs
and thorn bushes, covers the ground. However, to the north the dense vegetation
clears slightly, giving a view of a road that snakes through the oppressive
forest. To the east and west the forest seems as dark and dreary as ever.
name: Gloomy Forest

desc:
Huge oaks with thick overarching branches grow in this area of the forest. The
soil is dark and gnarled roots twist on the ground. The vegetation is dense,
especially to the north and west making it impossible to continue travel in
those directions. The faint rustling of leaves can be heard occasionally, like
small creatures moving amidst the dense vegetation.
name: Gloomy Forest

desc:
Inside the hollow, rotten trunk of this giant, fallen tree the wood has become
spongy and wet. The trunk forms an unusual passageway where a multitude of
mosses and fungi grows on the bottom, and worms and centipedes squirm in the
decaying wood. The trunk has partially collapsed in places, leaving small cracks
and holes in the ceiling and sides.
name: Huge, Rotten Trunk

desc:
Small animal trails wind their way through the forest. The trails are narrow and
light, and disappear here and there. Prints in the shape of boots are visible as
well, hinting that these trails are used by more than the occasional forest
animal. The footprints continue deeper into the forest to the north. While the
forest to the south is too dense for travel, a small hole can be seen in this
direction. It appears to be the exit from a massive fallen tree which is barely
noticeable due to the dark forest landscape. To the west the passage is wide
enough to be able to squeeze through.
name: Winding Animal Trails

desc:
The small hill is covered with grass in various colours such as green, red brown
and almost grey. Thick branches and underbrush obscure the way to the north, and
a dank smell and dampness seem to flow out of the forest from that direction. To
the east, at the very edge of the forest stands an especially large tree, rotten
pieces of bark sitting at its trunk.
name: Meadow along the Road

desc:
The only traversable routes through this dense part of Mirkwood are to the south
and west. The ground is slightly spongy, and a humid and chilly air flows in
from the north. Many of the thick vines hanging from the oak trees, that would
normally obstruct movement, have been cut off.
name: A Dense Forest

desc:
The feet of many small woodland animals have left their prints on this piece of
land. The trail weaves in and out of the dark trees, but appears to head north.
To the east, Mirkwood shows its true structure. Masses of thick vegetation block
any chance of travel there.
name: Wooded Game Trail

desc:
It is hard to be sure, not only because of the thickness of the forest, but also
because of the rapid growth of all the small plants and fungi on the forest
floor; yet it would appear that some kind of a trail leads east, toward the more
dense regions of Mirkwood.
name: The Dissipating Game Trail

desc:
Single footprints are visible here and there, converging onto a small path, and
continuing deeper into the dark woods to the north. Occasional sounds can be
heard from the forest gloom beyond the trees. Dense bushes make travel to the
south and west impossible. Pieces of bark and old dried twigs covers most of the
forest floor.
name: Dense Bushes

desc:
An old overgrown path runs through the area, coming from the south. It ends at a
large rock, where twigs and moss cover it, making it impossible to keep
following. The rock which was most likely placed here as a small navigational
landmark has a roughly triangular shape, and the sharpest corner points to the
west. In that direction the forest appears to open up a little bit.
name: A Strange Rock

desc:
This is the site of a small fortified outpost. Logs, branches and vines were
used to build obstructions and simple fortified walls among the trees. The crude
construction style suggests that this camp was built and inhabited by orcs. Some
scattered equipment lying on the ground look like they were dropped in a hurry.
Several strange marks cover the camp ground. Partly between the vines and one of
the walls is a big strong spider web, recently built; a couple of big raven
feathers are stuck in the web.
name: Deserted Orc Camp

desc:
The trees of the forest are now far more dominant. Massive firs and oaks grow in
abundance here, their crowns allowing no sunlight through to the ground. The
lack of sun has made the vegetation of the forest floor minimal, which makes
identifying and following the narrow path easy. To the north a wall of trees
prevents any travel in this direction.
name: Dense Forest of Mirkwood

desc:
The forest has given way to a vast bog. The ground is completely saturated with
water, making travelling very tiresome and extremely dangerous. The air is full
of sounds, as though to compensate for the void left by the sudden absence of
living trees: Only dead, tall trees tower up like pillars from the otherwise
quite clear marsh. The sounds join into a deceptively soothing melody, so
powerful that only the most focused traveller can resist its power and avoid
being luring into the marshy depths. To the east an impenetrable wall of
vegetation completely blocks any possibility of travel. To the north the
marshland appears to continue.
name: Edge of the Marsh

desc:
The trees in this area have thinned out dramatically. What trees there are have
a dull, grey appearance which could be due to the lack of those mosses and fungi
which cover the trees to the north-east. Many have dying branches, completely
devoid of leaves. Towards the south the same theme of decay appears to go on for
miles. The forest has become filled with other plant life, ranging from ferns
and grasses to, in some spots, swathes of flowers and patches of berry bushes.
To the north and east the forest begins to come alive again, the trees showing
leaves and having a characteristic black bark.
name: Withering Forest

desc:
The increase in elevation is rapid as the ground slopes heavily down to the
east. The forest floor is littered by roots and vines of various sizes. The
tall, ancient trees reach high into the sky and are so numerous that sunlight is
nearly completely blocked out. The almost total absence of light prevents small
vegetation from growing here. To the west, the forest appears to open up, while
to the north it becomes far more dense.
name: Deep Forest Prominence

desc:
Here, it seems as though the very earth and sky have succumbed to Mirkwood.
Looking up yields not a glimpse of anything beyond the branches which form an
entangled mass forty feet above the ground. On the forest floor, the ferns and
grasses twist and contort together, competing for every scare ray of sunlight.
name: Inner Depths of Mirkwood

desc:
One look at the razed remanants of the decorated flets tells you that wood elves
once resided here. In the middle of the encampment the trees were cleared,
allowing the sun's warm beams to shine down unimpeded. There, a garden was
planted; its flowers are now long dead and the soil is ridden with weeds. To the
west a massive oak door is set into the trunk of an ancient oak tree. No doubt,
the presence of Sauron has forced the inhabitants of this old elven place to
flee.
name: Abandoned Elven Encampment

desc:
The base of this ancient oak has been hollowed to form an escape shelter if the
need for one ever arises. The base of the tree has a circumference of twenty
feet. Inside this massive room there is little light, since the trunk is void of
any openings except for the oak door which serves as the entrance to this wooden
room. Along the north-western side of the room an intricate and elegant spiral
staircase travels up the sides of the trunk.
name: Inside an Oak

desc:
This wooden room is located approximately fifteen feet above the ground deep
inside of an old oak tree. The trunk of the tree has several holes around the
circumference of the tree. At this altitude one can easily peer through these
holes, making this room into a tree-top vantage point with views of the nearby
surroundings. To the west, a large hole has been made in the trunk of the oak.
name: Tree-top Vantage

desc:
Here the forest is composed primarily of tall ancient beech trees, though there
are also some oaks. The bark of these trees is dark as night, and some have rub
marks, betraying the presence of large animals. The trees are distorted and
twisted, their branches weaving together to form a canopy which allows barely
any sunlight through. To the east the forest appears to thin out and allow for
easier and a quicker pace.
name: Menacing Forest

desc:
The edge of this clearing is beginning to look more and more like the forested
area which surrounds it. Trees are now becoming numerous and have begun to grow
close together. While it is still easy to travel through, it has become more of
a challenge. To the west the forest turns as dark as night. To the south the
depths of Mirkwood are visible. Tall pines stand out above the tree line.
name: Edge of the Clearing

desc:
This clearing is well hidden by the maze of twisted and contorted trees which
lies to the north. The trees have separated enough to make crossing this area
easy. To the south the vegetation becomes denser, with bushes growing thickly
there.
name: Secret Clearing

desc:
This is a strange part of the forest. Rather than growing tall, reaching up to
the sky, the trees, both large and small, have instead twisted around each
other. It is often difficult to see where one tree begins and another ends.
Travel here is very hard. There is no path at all and it would be very easy to
loose one's bearings and stray ever deeper into this entangled, malicious-
looking wood.
name: The Twisted Trees

desc:
Numerous black, twisted, malicious-looking trees grow here. Their thick trunks
block the travelling in some directions, while in some others one may find paths
to go past them. All around, branches with sharp-looking thorns reach out as if
trying to harm anyone entering this forgotten grove. Above the entangled forest
a large oak is easily visible, standing tall on a slight incline to the west.
name: Twisted Black Trees

desc:
Tall oak trees cover this hilly area. There is an incline to the north where a
slope leads up to more dense forest. The incline has allowed light to break
through the forest canopy and thus enabled a multitude of plant life to flourish
on the forest floor. Ferns and grasses are prominent throughout these forest
hills. To the west and south the forest grows darker and more dense, blocking
the way.
name: Dense Forested Incline

desc:
The forest here is so dense that only the smallest of animals can live in it.
The forest floor sees very little light: most of the sun's rays are trapped by
the elaborate network of branches high above. The lack of sunlight means that
most normal woodland vegetation cannot grow here; only small fungi and the
hardiest of light grasses grown on a soil which is rich and dark.
name: Dark Dense Forest

desc:
The vegetation is overwhelmingly dense in this area. To the north and east there
is a large thicket which cannot be penetrated. The only option left for travel
is to enter the dark twisted forest to the south or to attempt the arduous
passage which the root-infested lands to the west present. A big thick log lies
here, stripped of all bark and marked with a few hacks from a sword or an axe.
name: Impenetrable Thicket

desc:
Atop of the forest hill stands a massive, decaying fir tree. This tree once
stood so high that only the eagles could gain the altitude needed to see its
crown from above. Now all that stands of it is a rotted shell. To the north-east
one can discern mountain ridges above the forest tree line. To the east the
vegetation begins to get thicker, almost giving the impression of being
impassable.
name: A Decaying Fir Tree

desc:
Very few rays of light are able to escape the forest canopy to touch upon this
dark and densely forested hill. Due to the lack of sunlight the ground is
desolate and free of vegetation. The only signs of life on the forest floor are
small animal tracks and the massive roots of the ancient trees which can be seen
reaching high to form the forest canopy at the top of this very hill.
name: The Dark Forest Hillside

desc:
Tall straight trees form these woods, close to the border of a rocky meadow to
the south. Further into the forest, the darkness becomes deeper, and the
denseness of the old oak trees and thorny shrubs prevents going north or east.
The shadows beyond the trees are home to many strange creatures.
name: Edge of the Forest

desc:
The forest to the south is lighter and more spacious. The edge of the forest is
close, and trees give way to a small meadow surrounding the ancient Old Forest
Road. To the north the woods are getting darker and damp, with twisted trees and
dense bushes blocking any movement. East and west of here, the forest is more
friendly, and travel in those directions is easy.
name: Near a Forest Meadow

desc:
A large rock stands in the middle of this overgrown meadow. Moss-covered trees
are clearly visible to the north: their branches have grown so thickly together
that it appears travel in this direction would be hard. The ground is somewhat
marshy, and springs a bit under your foot. To the east and west, the meadow
continues a bit farther before being swallowed by the dark forest.
name: Meadow North of the Road

desc:
This place is well inside Mirkwood, lacking any landmarks or signs of
civilisation. At some places though, odd shapes printed into tree barks remind
one that the forest is inhabited by ancient and powerful creatures. The trees
grow denser to the west, making travel in that direction too difficult. To the
north, the spaces get very narrow, but possible to traverse. The trees around
seem to move and reach toward travellers, making it hard to tell which direction
leads where.
name: Inside Mirkwood

desc:
The ground is mostly sandy here. The forest is made up of dead-looking trees
with no leaves left. To the east however are some more lively-looking ones. Some
spider webs hang from the trees, like the ghostly sails of some ethereal ship. A
small, almost non-existing trail leads towards a huge boulder standing at the
foot of a dark wall of trees.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
This part of the forest looks dead. The forest floor is covered by a great
number of what look like white sticks. On a closer look, they seem to be bones.
The ground is covered with them, though sometimes they are partly concealed by
dead leaves and roots. There is at least one complete skeleton here, wrapped in
spider-silk. To the west, a mass of vines forms an insurmountable wall.
name: Dead Forest along the Road

desc:
Occasionally one notices strange white stones, lying around between the roots of
this dark, dead forest. Squares of bark are missing on many of the trees, as if
they had been assaulted by some evil creatures. Innumerable spider webs hang
down between the trees. The forest continues toward east and west, but the way
south is blocked by an impregnable forest wall.
name: Sinister Forest

desc:
This dense forest of pines is very dark. The ground is sandy and covered in pine
needles. Lots of ferns grow in the humid sandy soil. Trails lead to the west,
through Mirkwood. One of them snakes between the very thick forest to the Old
Forest Road. To the west, the trees seem curiosly lifeless.
name: The Depths of Mirkwood

desc:
The vegetation in the marshland here is a mix of muddy trees, forest plants and
marsh ones. The marshy ground sinks readily under the least weight, threatening
to give way and leave the unsuspecting traveller to the dark deep water which
lies beneath the deceptive surface. And yet, the splendour of the different
colours of the mosses and fungi make the scene one of beauty as well as
deception.
name: Wooded Marshland Bog

desc:
This is the lowest and wettest part of the surrounding forest. Most of the
forest floor is soaked in a putrid liquid, oozing around the tree trunks. A
thick mist blankets the whole ground, making it impossible to see any drier
patches of ground, even if there were any. The trees around here are high and
smooth, and shaped as though trying to stay as far away from the ground as
possible, and the bushes are no more than a mass of grey, rotting twigs,
smothered by the bog.
name: A Forest Bog

desc:
Here at the edge of the marsh the terrain becomes more firm and secure, making
for easier going than that to the south. The pallet of colours has changed
dramatically from that of the marshlands to the south. Flourescent mosses and
fungi are now replaced by a twisted path of dark roots and vines coming from the
east. Far to the east a slight hill is visible.
name: Edge of the Marsh

desc:
Roots and vines twist together into an conglomerate of thick and rising
obstacles which make this area very hard to pass through. The mass rises in some
areas up to ten feet tall, while in others it dives deep below the level of the
forest floor. The rooted mass makes finding any visible path impossible. Looking
beyond the roots to the east, a rising hill is evident as well as an ancient fir
tree which rises high above its surroundings. The roots appear to continue to
the south and to the southwest where they will leech the resources of the
saturated marshland.
name: Amidst the Root Tangle

desc:
Roots and vines surround this room. The floor is muddy, treacherous to walk
upon, making leaving this room both dangerous and exhausting. Looking up all one
can see is the twisted forms of the roots and vines which dive deep into this
very room. These vines could perhaps be a convenient means to help an
unsuspecting traveller escape this underground chamber.
name: Within the Roots

desc:
Here lies a mass of roots, intertwined and distorted, which stretches from
Mirkwood in the east to the marshy lands lying to the west. The roots can be
seen diving deep into the forest floor and reappearing yards away. They make
traversing this terrain an incredible challenge. To the south a massive forest
wall blocks any possibility of travel in that direction.
name: Among the Twisted Roots

desc:
The humidity in the air has made mosses and fungi grow abundantly. Many of the
tree trunks are covered to a considerable height in black and dark-green moss,
and the bases of the trees are surrounded by patches of fungi and giant
mushrooms. None of the mushrooms have been disturbed or eaten by animals, and
they have strong distinct colours, seemingly indicating their poisonousness. The
denseness of the woods prevents going west or east, and travel to the south is
hampered by thick thorny bushes.
name: Among Moss-covered Trees

desc:
The ground slopes down to the east, nearing the edge of a marshy, depressed part
of the forest. The soggy ground is springy and makes a slight slushing sound
when walked upon. The proximity to the marshlands creates an extremely humid
atmosphere, causing the tree trunks to be wet, and many are starting to rot. To
the west, the ground slopes up and the forest becomes drier.
name: A Humid Forest

desc:
The gloom deepens in this part of Mirkwood forest. Movement is severely hampered
and no paths or other signs of civilisation are visible, except for small animal
trails that appear to go nowhere. The only landmark is the rising slope towards
higher ground to the east and south. Thick, entwined bushes form dark, thorny
walls that block any travel to the south and west. On both sides of the animal
trail the grass and ground has clear traces of old decaying animals, still
covered in strong sticky web.
name: Deep inside Mirkwood

desc:
The forest here is too dense to see very far in any direction. The surrounding
oak trees are ancient and crooked. Their trunks and branches twist and turn
around each other, forming small niches and impenetrable wooden walls that
prevent any movement except to the west and south. This is an excellent place
for small animals to build a nest, or as a shelter for anything that wants to
stay hidden. The ground is partly covered in thick white web that climbs its way
up on the trunks.
name: Dense, Black Forest

desc:
Thorny bushes are everywhere in this dense part of the forest. Scattered all
around on the forest floor, rotten pieces of bone slowly disintegrate. High up
in the trees, some very strange shapes seem to hang from the branches. The
canopy is not easily visible, as the many twisted branches, many more than
anywhere else in the forest, are densely interlocked, forming an odd sort of net
well below the level of the canopy. Suspended from a few of the twisted
branches, round spider webs of various sizes can be seen. The bushes prevent any
move to the north and the south. To the west, the branches that block the only
way through could easily be moved.
name: Deep in a Thicket

desc:
The spider lair is surrounded by walls of trees and web. Fallen trees and thorny
branches are packed together and held in place by thick chains of spider silk.
The ground is sticky and pulls on one's feet as one walks around. A foul stench
comes from a section of the lair to the west, and to the north the forest
becomes narrower and filled with shadows.
name: Woven Lair between the Trees

desc:
Old, tall trees reach upwards, their crowns disappearing into the green,
overgrown canopy. The forest floor is covered with huge roots, leaves and old
dried branches. A large, web-covered hole can be seen on the ground between a
few very thick trunks.
name: A Webbed Hole

desc:
Below the cavern dome enormous spider webs stretch out, covering the walls and
the slightly sloping floor. The webbed ground is very sticky, making it
difficult to walk fast. Webs are spun between the ceiling and floor, creating a
maze of walls and niches and pulling on anything going past. Towards the
south-east the floor slopes up and the webs become thicker, rising up to the
silken throne of the lord of this lair.
name: Giant, Webbed Cavern

desc:
Black, oppressive trees stand tall: watching, listening... Their trunks rise
high into the darkness where the intermingled crowns twist around each other
creating a full, shadowy canopy. Thick masses of vines, low-hanging branches,
roots and other parts of the underbrush form tangled barriers, leaving no choice
for a traveller but to continue to the north or west. The ground ascends
steadily to the west.
name: Black Forest

desc:
Winding its way through the dark and dense forest, a meandering passageway runs
in a wide arc around a rise south-west from here. A thick layer of rotten mulch
covers the way, and the surrounding trees lean inwards, as if trying to swallow
these last passable parts of the woods. The occasional spider web has been woven
between the black and dark-grey trees.
name: Black Forest

desc:
Tall, wide, twisted trees are all around, leaving only just enough room to move
below the dense branches. What little room is still left on the ground is being
conquered by dead leaves, twigs, mosses, roots and lichens which have obviously
been piling up for a long time. The sky is completely blocked out by the thick
canopy - darkness pervades Mirkwood.
name: Black Forest

desc:
The surrounding forest looks as dark and twisted as it does everywhere else, as
it continues its implacable struggle to overwhelm the narrow pathway that barely
allows passage through this area. Roots, branches and fallen trunks make
movement hard. There is a knoll to the southeast, forcing travellers to go
around it to the left or right, and so luring and leading them further and
further into the woods. The thick canopy looms above, engulfing everything in
perpetual darkness.
name: Black Forest

desc:
Twisted, strange-looking trees grow in an isolated small group. They look like a
band of wicked old men who have frozen into positions expressive of eternal
torment and pain. Outside this creepy grove, steep slopes climb upwards to the
east and south, their surfaces a mess of trees, roots and other entangled
vegetation.
name: Black Forest

desc:
A thick blanket of moss covers large parts of the ground, concealing the small,
twisting paths below the looming trees. Dark bushes with needle-sharp thorns
grow all over the area, trying to conquer any open space there still is. A
foreboding, unusually total silence has taken over the area, especially the
western part where it seems to become almost palpable. One can continue from
here towards the west, south and east, where the forest looks less oppressive.
name: Black Forest

desc:
Amid the dark trees a steep, vine-covered cliff rises to the west. The vines are
dark and leathery, possibly also sturdy enough to provide support if one was to
attempt to scale the cliff. A patch of entwined trees grows to the south,
forcing anyone to travel around it; with the cliff to the west, this leaves east
as the only possible route.
name: Steep Cliff Deep in a Twisted Forest

desc:
Unnaturally dark trees grow all around, gripping at each other's branches and
twisting around each other, creating a wall to the woods that greatly restricts
both vision and movement. Whereas most forests would be filled with sounds from
its inhabitants, not much can be heard in here, as if whatever inhabitants there
are prefer to stay as silent as possible.
name: Black Forest

desc:
Black hawthorns are pressing in on all sides, as if trying to create a prison
amidst the twisted trees. Among the trees, dark vines grow, further hampering
sight and movement which the trees already hindered very well on their own. The
soft cover on the ground and the dense vegetation muffles all sounds, making the
forest unusually quiet.
name: Black Forest

desc:
Great, dark trees stand tall, blocking out any sight of the sky above with their
dense branches, widespread crowns and wide, gnarled boles. There are some big
boulders on the ground, surrounded by dense, sharp-leafed bushes. To the east
and south the ground tilts down somewhat, trees and dense undergrowth blocking
travel down the slopes.
name: Black Forest

desc:
A dark mass of vines grows densely around the area. The vines reach down from
the canopy, then snake around the twisted trees, and finally entangle themselves
in the shrubs, creating impassable barriers. It is possible to move to the east,
but to the west a vine-covered cliff rises from the ground, blocking the way.
Southwards the ground slopes downwards, though the vines make it impossible to
tell how much, or how far until the ground levels out again.
name: Black Forest

desc:
A small open passageway runs through the room from an outdoor to the west to an
innerdoor to the east. Other than that, the room contains beds, beds and beds;
There are two bunk beds next to each other both on the northern and on the
southern wall, with three beds on top of each other in every one of them. The
beds and the walls look grey and old, and at parts they are covered in spider
webs. There are neither windows nor other furniture in the room.
name: Sleeping Quarters

desc:
Judging by the the long table with benches on both sides, some cabinets along
the walls and a sturdy oven in the corner, this room is clearly an old kitchen.
It has not been in use for ages though, as all surfaces are covered with dust
and numerous spider webs have been woven around the room. Planks have been
nailed to cover the windows. There are two doors, a sturdy one leading out and
another leading deeper into the cottage.
name: Old Kitchen

desc:
The sea of grass is surrounded by the dark, twisted and inwards-reaching woods
on all sides except to the east, where the waves of grass continue in defiance
of the high and looming trees. The forest to the north is too dense and twisted
to allow any travelling. To the west stands an old moss-covered cottage. Even
when its roof and grey walls are growing bushes and mould, and vines have
entangled themselves with it, the walls made of sturdy logs look still intact.
There is a small overgrown path which leads from the cottage to the south where
something that could be a road can be seen through the trees.
name: An Old Cottage on the Forest Glade

desc:
This room is nothing but a hole of few square yards dug into the ground. There
are old partly broken shelves on every wall, small ladders that lead upwards to
a hatch on the ceiling and and old barrel in one corner of the room. The walls
behind the shelves and the floor of the room are of the dark soil.
name: Dark, Musty Cellar

desc:
Long grass stretches out all around this treeless opening, which is like a sea
of stillness within the twisted forest. The sky with all its wonders is visible
above, but this privilege is of no great extent, for the dark forest looms close
to the north and south, standing there as densely as anywhere with just barely
enough space between the dark boles to make room for passage.
name: Forest Glade

desc:
Narrow, winding, seldom-travelled trails lead through the dense and gloomy woods
further into the forest somewhere to the west while to the south the forest
opens up into a clearing of some sort. To the north a wall of dark oaks rises
above the other trees, blocking out whatever lies north of it.
name: Gloomy Woods

desc:
Deep within the dark woods is a dead-end formed by the entwined trees. The
forest in the only direction out from here, east, is not much different than
elsewhere, except that the trees do not grow so closely together as to prevent
passage for everything but small animals.
name: Gloomy Woods

desc:
A black, weird-looking rock stands in the middle of this dark grove. The rock is
about ten feet high, with two veins of ore visible. A huge, almost two feet
thick root has entwined itself around the rock as if holding it fast to the
ground like an arm of the forest protecting its own treasures. All around, sharp
branches reach out from giant gnarled trunks standing next to each other. An
abundance of vines growing between the trunks create a wall that severely limits
travel options.
name: Grove of Wicked Plants

desc:
Below the dark canopy of Mirkwood, masses of corpse-grey trees are leaning
towards the small opening in a perfect unison. The trees have lost their
vitality but still stand as massive as ever, darkening the surroundings with
their thick trunks. Old, gnarled roots snake across the ground, making walking
difficult and riding impossible. Some of the trees are gnawed by ivy, some of
them have fallen over and now lie rotting on the ground. The rotting trees seem
to have been disturbed recently. Patches of the forest floor are riddled with
traces of a struggle, some of the roots have old cuts in them and the ground
looks disturbed as if it had been dug in recently.
name: Grove of Wicked Plants

desc:
Like a tightening circle, the plants of this dark sinister grove seem to shift
around as if trying to reach for travellers. Black shrubbery covered in nasty
sharp thorns, oozing vines thick as a bear's neck and towering trunks have all
entangled together, leaving only a few exits open below and between the plants.
The ground is shadowed by the continuous canopy of Mirkwood which filters out
most of the light that could ever reach this place.
name: Grove of Wicked Plants

desc:
Atop this rocky cliff one could see far and wide, were the ever-present twisted
trees not reaching high above and thus blocking the view. The cliff prevents
further passage in all directions save to the west, unless one wants to test the
strength of the plants along the steep cliff wall leading down. To the west the
forest becomes darker and more foreboding.
name: On Top of a Cliff

desc:
Atop this rocky cliff one could see far and wide, were the ever-present twisted
trees not reaching high above and thus blocking the view. The cliff prevents
further passage in all directions save to the west, unless one wants to test the
strength of the plants along the steep cliff wall leading down. To the west the
forest becomes darker and more foreboding.
name: On Top of a Cliff

desc:
A heavily forested small ridge rises up from its surroundings. The malicious-
looking twisted trees stand close together in a circle, like a crown on top of
the highest part of the ground. All around, the endless rows of thick trunks
dotted by thorny bushes and blanketed by an eerie gloom make the navigation
hard.
name: Among the Twisted Trees

desc:
The canopy high above blocks most sun or moon light, but the oak trunks stand
far apart and the branches are not as twisted and thick as elsewhere. The widely
spaced forest continues to the south and west, but to the north and east the
density of trees and bushes increases so much that travel in those directions is
impossible. The ground slopes up towards the west, soon reaching the top of a
small hill.
name: A Spacious Forest

desc:
Here a small, lonely, wooded hill rises out of Mirkwood. In contrast to the rest
of the forest, the trees here do not completely block the view, and several
prominent landmarks are visible from this vantage point. The dark, fir-covered
slopes of the Mountains of Mirkwood dominate most of the northern skies. Going
east is the easy way off the hill, as all other directions are either blocked by
dense bushes or require climbing down a steep cliff.
name: High Ground in the Forest

desc:
This small, enclosed area of the forest looks like the place where the spiders
leave the equipment and other inedible goods they find on their victims. The
ground is blood-soaked and dark red, a testament to the horrors that have taken
place here, and nothing wants to grow on it. The trees are blackened and old, as
if scorched by fire. Their branches twist and turn, and only to the south is a
gap large enough to squeeze through.
name: Enclosure of Black Trees

desc:
This dark, narrow tunnel between the huge trunks and interwoven branches has
sticky walls made of spider webs. There are corpses and skeletons of small
animals gotten stuck, and occasional scrape marks in few places. The dark grey
ceiling is just above and some thick branches penetrating the endless webs are
so low that creatures taller than a dwarf have to crawl forward.
name: Dark, Narrow Tunnel

desc:
The oak trees growing here are thick and ancient. Most of the trees lean inwards
- whether that comes from their own weight or the shifting ground is unclear. A
permanent dark atmosphere pervades the forest, making the trees look like giants
rising up, ready to strike. Their branches touch one another at a low height,
twisting around and hugging each other, creating a sort of low wooden roof. The
ground here is sloped and the only possible exits are a open path north, or
downhill to the south, the way to the east being blocked by thick, twisted
branches.
name: Among the Looming Trees

desc:
The trees here are ancient and bent in this gloomy and humid forest. Some are
dark and rotten, and overgrown by younger bushes. Like everything in Mirkwood
that grows old and weak, they feed the newest generation. Here and there lie old
stone blocks, now covered in moss, that once perhaps formed part of an outpost.
Everywhere around, evil has seized power over this place, proud of its influence
on what happens in its lands. Fallen trees and thorny bushes seem to prevent
travel in the eastern or southern directions.
name: Old Stones in the Dark Forest

desc:
This is a lighter part of the forest, with more space between the trees, making
it easy to move around. The area appears to be frequently used by the animals
inhabiting these woods, as many small animal trails twist between the trees.
Around some trails, however, scratches in the bark of trunks suggest dangerous
creatures around. The widely-spaced forest continues to the north. To the east
the spaces get narrower but still traversable, while to the west and south the
forest is too dense to move through.
name: Crossing of Animal Trails

desc:
The trees in this part of the forest stand close together, making it difficult
to move around. To the west, the forest becomes lighter and the spaces wider,
but in all other directions the branches are twisting and entwined. This creates
impassable barriers to the north and south, but to the east it is possible to
squeeze through a narrow passage. Coming back from that direction might not be
as easy, though.
name: Narrow Space in the Forest

desc:
This part of the forest is big and green and luscious, its air deep and misty
and filled with noises. The mist so thick it leaves a wet surface to anything or
anyone that would move through here. The unmistakable smell of composting leaves
fills the air. To the south a rock covered in moss blocks the way. An old,
almost fully overgrown path comes from the west and leads north, towards an old
trunk.
name: An Old Overgrown Path

desc:
The roughly circular clearning is surrounded by twisted, ancient trees among
which deep shadows seem to loom and move. Many of the trees are cracked and
broken, stains that look like dried blood can be seen on their old trunks and
sharp branches.. The floor is littered with debris and sharp wood, there also
are remnants of small animals but also those of humanoid origin. Perhaps it was
an orc or an elf; now nothing more remains of them than their bones littering
the floor.
name: Amidst Twisted Trees

desc:
The dense and continuous canopy of the forest, the result of hanging vines,
thick branches and giant-sized black leaves, spreads in all directions engulfing
the area in darkness. The undergrowth is dense with patches of ivy and shrubs
growing all over. Some rocks and stones lie scattered on the ground, mostly
covered in dark moss and lichens. An oppressing silence pervades this place.
name: Gloomy Forest

desc:
The trees in this area are different than any other trees in the nearby
surroundings. They are a mixture of oaks and firs, reaching far into the sky.
The bark of these trees is an ashen grey, with lichens populating the outer
surface of the trunk. The trees have twisted together in a manner which has
created a massive forest wall, which is practically impenetrable. The branches
of the trees twist overhead, making impossible the climbing of this wall. The
ground here is warm and moist, as the evil forest protects its roots from the
sun. A small glimpse of light can seen penetrating the southern wall of forest.
name: A Twisted Wall

desc:
Here the dark woods have been forced to loosen their grip on the environment,
causing an almost serene glade to open in the midst of the more malevolent
forest surrounding it. The high grass and the absence of gnarled roots to trip
unwary travellers makes this area far easier to pass through than the forest
around it. The glade continues to the west while the forest takes over to the
north and south, though in the south the forest isn't quite as dense.
name: Forest Glade

desc:
Here, on the western side of a great meadow, bright green tufts of grass stand
out against the brown brush and dead undergrowth surrounding the trees to the
north and west. To the south, a road makes its way through the forest. At some
places, barely noticeable, very old trails start and end without any obvious
purpose, but they all seem to lead from the northwest to the east.
name: Forest at the Edge of a Meadow

desc:
The forest has overcome all surrounding terrain. The sun is unable to penetrate
this deep dark world. Evil lurks in these areas in many forms, and the darkness
seems to draw its strength from a foreign power. A very old path, invaded by
barely younger trees, wanders between the trunks, leading to a less dense forest
to the west, and into the deeps of Mirkwood to the east; brambles and fallen
trees prevent move to the south and north.
name: Path between the Trees

desc:
This part of the forest smells strongly of rotting vegetation. Branches have
fallen all about here, their twisted branchlets snapping underfoot as you walk.
Bones and various debris lie scattered on the forest floor. The bones have
obvious traces of charcoal, indicating that someone has tried to burn them. The
trees grow together very tightly in all directions but west.
name: Among the Rotten Branches

desc:
Rough, sharp gravel covers the ground in this round opening between and below
the cliffs, making it difficult to stand or walk on. The gravel varies in
colour, from light red to deep grey, creating an odd contrast to the black,
looming cliffs. The visibility from down here is poor and all that can be seen
are steep rock walls towering up on all sides, save to the west, where lush
vegetation covers the rock wall.
name: Bottom of a Cliffwall

desc:
Various trees grow here: there are oaks, birches and yews, all standing side by
side forming a densely-grown forest where movement is rather difficult.
Characteristically for Mirkwood the thick, gnarled trunks are dark, much darker
than these trees usually are elsewhere on Arda. Vines and branches hang from the
canopy. The ground is rather flat except to the east where it rises steeply
upwards.
name: Thick Forest

desc:
Thick, large, white web covers the ceiling where three different mummified
bodies have been spun up within the web. Like the underside of a hammock, the
large webs hang from the roof, camouflaging the exact shape and size of the
chamber. The extensive floor is of clean, flat rock. The northern part is
roughly a foot higher than the south and as such forms some sort of plateau.
name: A Webbed Spider Lair

desc:
This stinking chamber is filled with multiple hazy walls of sticky spider webs,
limiting the visibility and making it difficult to move without getting stuck.
Some cocoons are hanging from silky threads, occasionally swinging a little bit
among and above the webs. Parts of dismembered corpses, empty husks of deceased,
and clusters of huge spider eggs have sunk into the grey slime that covers all
the surfaces.
name: A Horrifying Chamber

desc:
An abundance of silky webs fill this underground chamber from the floor to the
ceiling. Few grotesque cocoons of different sizes hang in the air; some look
grey and dry while others look stickier and their colours contain splashes of
green and brown as well. Zig-zagging among and between sticky webs, it is hard
to tell which way leads where.
name: Chamber Covered by Spider Webs

desc:
Well hidden by the thick, downwards-reaching branches and evergreen shrubs, this
small rise in the forest serves as an excellent watch post for anyone who wants
to watch the road passing by just to the north. The broken twigs, the flattened
undergrowth, and the remnants of someone's meals suggest that this place is in
regular use. In all directions save to the south the ground slopes downwards,
making it highly uncertain if one can get back here after descending.
name: Hideout in a Gloomy Forest

desc:
Several tiny tunnels come together here, but thick webs and cave-ins have
blocked all the tunnels except the one leading to the south. The floor shows
signs of regular and recent usage although no exit to the forest above can be
seen. The high density of spider webs and the many roots lying around hinder
movement.
name: Rough, Earthy Tunnel

desc:
A rough, dug-out tunnel stretches north. The tunnel is far from straight and
slopes up and down, like a giant animal burrow. The tunnel does not appear to be
used as a nest, but rather for concealed transportation. Tree roots stick out of
the ceiling and walls, and webs are spun across sections of the tunnel,
hindering movement and ensnaring the careless. To the south, a cave-in has
occurred, blocking that route. A hole in the ceiling leads to the forest above,
but it is too slippery to climb up.
name: Winding Earthy Tunnel

desc:
Thick brambles and fallen trees have closed off this secluded area from the
surrounding forest. The gloom is deep, and the forest canopy thicker than
elsewhere. The ground is covered by numerous animal trails, wider and deeper
than normal, as if the animals dragged something along with them. The trails
lead to a hole in the ground, large enough to pass through, leading to a very
steep tunnel. In the wide canopy occasional webbing can be seen, long thick webs
hanging straight down from the tree-tops bearing witnessing to the presence of
spiders.
name: Secluded Animal Trail

desc:
Dead leaves, plants and twigs litter the ground, with some branches jutting up
through the mass like the rib bones of a bear. The dark and gnarled branches are
blotched with lighter patches of lichen and fungi of different sizes and
colours. The surrounding forest grows into a wall of tall oaks to the east,
beyond which a glade can be glimpsed, and to the south where one cannot find any
path between the twisting trees, vines and bushes where one could pass.
name: Dark Woods

desc:
Under the canopy of the tall, high-reaching trees, a rarely-trodden path winds
its way in a confusing manner between and around the trees of the dark woods. To
the north and south the trees have grown their dark boles and gnarled branches
so tightly that no trespassing is possible through the living wall of dark
vegetation.
name: Dark Woods

desc:
Ancient oak trees covered with dark mosses, lichen and fungi stand tall, their
dense crowns spreading high and wide, effectively blocking out the sky. The
passage that is heading in an east-west direction through this area could be
called a tunnel, so dark is it, and so densely grow the trees and the
undergrowth on either side. To the north the vine-covered ground rises upwards,
steeply enough to prevent passage up the slope.
name: Dark Woods

desc:
A very long, narrow tunnel opens up here. The walls are just raw rock that
shifts in colour, all varying from pitch black to bone white. The rock is cold
to the touch and the sharp edges looks almost hostile as the walls stand so
close together. No light comes in through the exit from the north, the air is
very damp and every breath you take thickens in your lungs. The ceiling is high
above and large smooth stalagmites hang a few feet down from the roof.
name: Narrow Tunnel

desc:
The floor is covered with material coming from rocks, crushed over and over
again to make a smooth black sand. The walls are rough and do not have the same
finish as the floor, as if the work was abruptly stopped for some reason. Along
all the walls are deep cracks running from floor to ceiling, varying in width.
On various places iron rods have been hammered into the cracks.
name: Rocky Tunnel

desc:
The Old Forest Road has not been used for a very long time, but it is still in
very good shape here. Small breaks, which allow limited travel off the road, can
be seen to the north and south. A faint reddish trail runs between the breaks
across the road. Ancient trees tower over the road stopping any hope of direct
sunlight breaking through. The road continues to the east and west.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
Most of the Old Forest Road is intact here. However, some of the stones have
come loose from the surface of the road and now lie scattered along the
roadside. Otherwise the road is in very good condition for travelling on foot or
on horseback. The crowns of the trees to the north reach out over the road
casting it in deep shadow. The forest blocks the way to the south.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
Over the years wind and rain has washed away small parts of the road. Fallen
branches and leaves lie undisturbed on the road. Despite the flaws in the road's
surface, travel along the route remains quick and easy. The branches and crowns
of the trees lining the road are blocking out most of the sky.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
The road makes a sharp turn to the north and west here. Some fallen trees can be
seen and need to be avoided. Here and there on the road, roots of the massive
trees lining it breaks the paved surface. The place has been cast into deep
shade by the thick canopy overhead and little can be heard save for the forest
creaking.
name: Bend in the Old Forest Road

desc:
The road stretches from this bend to the east and south. The road surface has
deteriorated leaving a thin layer of gravel over the remains of the paved road.
To the north, it is impossible to force a path into the forest because the trees
stand too close to each other. To the west, a dense thicket seems to have fallen
under heavy darkness, and the shifting branches seem to form grotesque shapes
that defy closer examination.
name: Bend in the Old Forest Road

desc:
The road through Mirkwood runs straight to the east and west. On the ground,
fallen branches and wilted leaves lie scattered, covering roots that encroach
onto the road from the surrounding forest. Several small ravines in the road's
surface seem to have been made by erosion. Flanking the road, black yews and
beeches rise tall, their branches reaching out above the road to cover the sky.
The road is kept in deep shadow and the only sound around here is the distant
chattering of small animals.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
On the ground there are obvious tracks of life. There are some blood stains
leading south beneath a half-built guard house that has been erected between
three massive black oak trees. All directions, except south which leads toward a
road, seem to have been cleared at some point but are now choked off due to the
forest undergrowth having vigorously grown in.
name: Under a Half-built Guardhouse

desc:
This mountain forms part of the foothills of the great mountains of Mirkwood.
Higher mountains dominate the skyline to the north and east. The height and
relative scarcity of trees allow for an unobstructed view over a great expanse
of the forest. This could have made for an excellent place to keep watch for
approaching enemies but the dense, green canopy of Mirkwood makes it impossible
to see what is moving at ground level.
name: Mountainside

desc:
The forest comes to an abrupt halt here and gives way to an open mountainside to
the north. The western flank of the valley is marked with a huge pile of scree
and debris, the result of a landslide that began high in the mountains of
Mirkwood. Evergreen fir trees thrive in this shady valley, their fallen needles
and discarded pine cones forming a blanket across the rocky ground.
name: Dark Forest Valley

desc:
Huge, dark, twisted trees grow inside this shadowy part of Mirkwood. Because the
bushes and undergrowth are mostly absent, there is enough space between the
trees to make the travelling, and even riding, relatively easy. The forested
pathway leads to the north and south.
name: Shadowy Forest

desc:
The trunks of the trees, gnarly roots, bushes, and the ground itself are all
shadowed by the thick canopy above. The darkness shrouds the forest in a gloomy
embrace, and it is almost impossible to see whether it is day or night. The
forest consists mostly of ancient, thick firs and pines.
name: Gloomy Forest

desc:
Large beech trees stand here draped in a curtain of vines. Creepers and vines
hang from every branch with their long tendrils brushing the ground as they sway
in the breeze. For a change, the trees seem to be losing their battle with the
forest as vines twist and bend around the trunks as if trying to strangle them.
There is a palisade and beyond it wooden structures visible to the south.
name: Vine-covered Forest

desc:
The ledge goes on to the east and west, and it looks like it is possible to
reach a higher ledge from here. Namely there is a tunnel in the wall that
ascends steeply and seems to turn slightly west. On the ground at this fork,
there are some scratch marks and loose moss on the rock. Perhaps some animal
lives nearby or has passed by recently.
name: Rocky Ledge

desc:
A wide ledge, littered with loose rocks and fallen scree, stretches to the east
and west. There seems to be a higher cliff edge visible above the canopy of the
forest. Thin vines and ivy grow up along the cliff, but they do not look strong
enough to hold even a hobbit's weight.
name: Rocky Ledge

desc:
This cliff edge amongst the forest canopy is covered by decaying leaves and
rotten branches. Some of the branches of the trees reach the ledge, hindering
passage while also allowing squirrels and other animals to travel freely between
the nearby trees and the narrow ledge. There is sickly green, slimy fungi
growing on the cliff and moss growing on the decomposed earth. A shred of the
sky is visible through the canopy.
name: Rocky Ledge

desc:
Vertical cliffs on two sides form the walls of this shaft. The cliffs prevent
direct sunlight from reaching this deep crack, but a thin shred of sky is
visible above. The dark, narrow shaft leads out onto a ledge to the west. It is
also possible to descend onto a lower ledge via a steep tunnel within the rock
face of the southern cliff wall.
name: Narrow Shaft in a Cliff

desc:
A rocky ledge lies just below the forest canopy, which continues to the south
and west. There are mountains to the north, but travelling in that direction is
not possible. The cliff continues to the east. A few saplings and a lot of vines
grow on the partially rocky shelves as well as the smaller ledges below this
larger ledge.
name: Rocky Ledge

desc:
The shaft ends abruptly at a vertical lichen-covered wall. There is a small
niche at the end of the shaft, which forms a shelter from rain and wind.
Decaying leaves and branches lie on a thin layer of earth. Some small pebbles
have been piled up near the niche, and in the niche itself there is a shelter
built from branches, leaves, and ropes of vine.
name: End of a Narrow Shaft

desc:
The cliff drops steeply to the west, its surface smooth and slippery. The only
way off this ledge is to follow it north or south, or to try and squeeze into a
narrow crack in the face of the cliff. Green moss grows on the cliff and vines
have crawled here from the canopy beneath. Strange fungi, mottled in shades of
yellow, have found a home here on the side of the cliff.
name: Fork on a High Ledge

desc:
The cliff that this ledge clings to bends sharply from east to north. To the
south-west, the view shows a great expanse of Mirkwood's dark foliage from
above. A small trickle of water, barely enough to wet the rocks and certainly
not enough for a drink, flows down from the higher cliff and drips down on the
forest.
name: Corner on a High Ledge

desc:
This ledge clings precariously to the side of the mountain and continues along
it to the east and west from here. The ledge narrows considerably at some points
and shattered fragments of scree cover it. Over its side there is nothing but
the green canopy of Mirkwood some distance below, and beneath that the hard
forest floor.
name: Narrow Ledge

desc:
This ledge seems dangerously unstable. Wide cracks and fractures have opened up
in the mountain side and cut clean across the ledge, splitting it into a series
of small, crumbling shelves. None of the fractures have yet become too wide to
not comfortably step over, but it is not clear how long it will remain this way.
To the south, the great green canopy of Mirkwood spreads out as far as the eye
can see.
name: Fractured Ledge

desc:
A rocky ledge turns sharply towards the north here, following the contour of the
mountain. It is narrow, and the forest floor lies far below. A sprawling green
canopy of treetops stretches out endlessly in all directions below, save where
the dark mountain thrusts up to obscure the view.
name: Corner on a Ledge

desc:
At the end of this very high cliff, there is a clear view of the Mountains of
Mirkwood to the east, and smaller peaks to the west and north. The green forest
canopy is almost level with this ledge. If great care is taken, moving south
along the ledge seems possible while the vertical face of the cliff might
provide opportunity for the daring to climb down into the forest below.
name: End of a High Ledge

desc:
The ledge gets wider here, and there are lots of loose boulders and rocks lying
on it. Some of the boulders are broken into pieces, as if they fell from great
heights. There are several dried, brown stains on one of the boulders and on the
ledge around it.
name: Stony Precipice

desc:
This mountain, an outlier of the greater Mountains of Mirkwood to the east,
rises over a hundred feet above the canopy. The ledge clinging to its face is
forced into a sharp bend here, and at the very corner, a large section of it has
collapsed.
name: Turn upon a Precipice

desc:
A rugged mountainside rises steeply to the south and west. The boulders on this
shelf, cracked, shattered and fragmented, lie where they landed when they
tumbled down from the heights above. To the west, beneath an overhang, a darker
opening against the shadows seem to hint at a cave. The shelf narrows into a
ledge to the east and disappears around a sharp curve on the mountain.
name: Boulder-strewn Shelf

desc:
This cave has the rugged and uneven walls of a natural cavern, but the floor is
surprisingly even, as if some effort has gone into making it more habitable.
There is a smouldering pile of charred wood and ashes upon the floor in the
centre of the cave, indicative of a recent presence here. There are some bones
and pieces of fur dumped in a depression behind a boulder just outside the
entrance; this is likely serving as a refuse pit.
name: Shallow Cave

desc:
The shrouded, dark forest is veiled in shadows and gloom. Long, thin branches
droop from the trees, as if grasping at the forest floor. Moving through the
forest is not easy, as the trees grow close to each other, and their branches
intertwine. The soil is dark and covered by decaying leaves and needles.
name: Shrouded Forest

desc:
The trees of the forest form a natural passage here, offering an easy way to
travel to the east and south. The forest in the other directions consist of
impenetrable thickets and tangles. The trees have formed a low arch with their
branches, under which a man atop a horse would be a narrow fit. A lot of beard
moss and grey-white lichen hang from the trees.
name: Passage in Mirkwood

desc:
The dense forest blocks passage in all directions except to the south. Thorny
thickets grow amongst towering, gnarled trunks, further hindering travel. A
carpet of decaying brown leaves covers the ground. The sky is almost completely
hidden by the canopy, naught but a gap here and there reveals the world beyond.
name: Dead-end in Mirkwood

desc:
The pine trees in this part of the forest are almost black in colour. The ground
is covered by a thick mat of decaying needles. Fungi thrive on the forest floor,
sprouting among the needles in dense clumps. The branches and the trunks of the
trees are covered by beard moss swaying slowly in the wind. A maze of the
branches seem to continue endlessly upwards.
name: Deep in Mirkwood

desc:
A very small clearing exists here in a tree thicket. The surrounding trees are a
mixture of oak and elm. Acorns and leaves cover the ground and rustle with any
movement across them. Moss and lichen have slowly started growing up the trees
here, but they are a darker shade of colour than they would typically be. The
forest is very thick, but small breaks in the umbrage can be seen to the north.
name: Dense Forest

desc:
The gnarled but powerful trees in this section of the forest have begun to
wither and die. Light can only reach the ground in thin, weak patches through
the leafless, yet dense, canopy of thick branches above. Broken and decaying
branches lie scattered on the forest floor, slowly rotting away. There is
neither sign nor sound of animals here, as if the wildlife knows better than to
pass through this region of Mirkwood.
name: Withering Copse

desc:
Some of the trees here show signs that they have not been growing for as long as
the surrounding forest. A huge variety of trees grow here; from birches, to
beeches, to oaks, and even some evergreens. A larger break in the canopy in the
south can be seen. Apparently, the forest was partially cleared in the past, but
it has since started to regrow.
name: Wandering in the Forest of Mirkwood

desc:
A large pine tree with dark-brown bark and emerald-green needles is here,
towering above the surrounding forest. The vast expanse of its massive branches
has cast a wide pool of shade upon the ground beneath it, where nothing save a
few short, stunted shrubs have dared to grow. A thick blanket of moss encircles
the ground around the base of tree, where it continues to grow several feet up
the massive trunk.
name: At a Tall Pine Tree

desc:
The woods here are made up of various types of trees, beneath which is a dense
layer of undergrowth. Thick, heavy tree foliage can be seen above and allows no
light to break through it. This makes the already dark forest appear even darker
than the surrounding forest. It is difficult to navigate through this stretch of
forest and one could easily end up getting lost while attempting to do so.
name: Black Woods

desc:
Inside this small and sodden cavern, there are a lot of roots growing through
the walls. The exception is the wall to the east, which looks like it might be
about to collapse. Water rushes in through a small opening among the roots above
and splashes onto the sludgy ground.
name: Small Sodden Cavern

desc:
Water wells out from a small hole in a wall made by the mountains to the north
and gathers here in a small pool. The air here seems slightly less stifling than
in the surrounding forest. A steep mountain slope rises up to the west, with the
forest closing in from the south and east, with the latter direction being
impassable due to a recent landslide. Closer to the mountains, the forest and
vegetation begins to thin out and makes it slightly easier to navigate through.
name: A Small Forest Front

desc:
Large quantities of bushes grow here, all closely packed together. The majority
of the bushes seem to be of a thornless variety, for not a single thorn is
visible throughout the whole grove. Black, half-rotten fungi are growing amongst
the bushes.
name: Entangled Wood

desc:
In this part of the forest, the trees seem to move when unwatched. Mammoth roots
break the surface of the earth and seem to search for something to ensnare along
with branches that bend down menacingly. As faint traces of wind rustle through
the branches, the trees seem to be murmuring between them.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
A prominent mountain on the northern flank of the sloping ground is vaguely
visible behind the thickets of the forest. Rocky patches between the giant trees
have been smoothed through centuries of exposure to wind and rain. Not a single
sapling or bush grows on the rocks, only some grey lichen and dark moss.
name: Forest along the Mountains

desc:
Great boughs spread out overhead, entwining themselves together. So thickly have
they been tangled that no light can pierce the canopy they form. From out of
this darkness, faint rustling noises can be heard. Where these noises are coming
from, whether the faint movement of the wind or from twisted creatures who live
in this forest, can not easily be determined.
name: Twisted Forest

desc:
The forest ends abruptly to the east and gives a view of the huge, dark
mountains of Mirkwood. Many sharp rocks have fallen from the cliff to the east
and have found their way down to the forest floor. Layers of scree have built up
over many years causing the mossy ground to crunch underfoot. The oppressive
forest to the west of here is very dense, and it is not possible to wind one's
way through the forest in that direction.
name: Battered Forest

desc:
The dark mountains of Mirkwood loom ominously beyond the rising, tangled forest
to the east, where a slope ascends very steeply. A lot of loose debris and scree
have accumulated on the slope, making a climb impossible. Rocks, boulders, and
stones of different sizes litter the forest floor and hamper travel.
name: Battered Forest

desc:
This small, flat summit is located atop the dark mountains of Mirkwood. Steep
cliffs on the western and southern sides drop off to the forest floor below.
This whole area is devoid of any plant life, with even the hardier varieties
having failed to find a place to grow amongst the cold, dark rock.
name: High Cliff

desc:
Small, dark and twisted trees struggle to grow on this inhospitable
mountainside. The strong winds that sweep across these peaks have twisted the
trees into grotesque shapes resembling that of malformed humanoids. The trees
shift and groan, and some almost seem to be stalking through the dark, dense
forest. Rocks and boulders create more shadowy places in the forest. One of the
boulders is huge and completely covered by moss and lichen.
name: Dark Mountains

desc:
This cavern inside the mountains serves as the lair of something large and
carnivorous judging by chewed and cracked bones on the ground. The cavern is
high and wide enough to let even the largest animal enter; which species has
made this place home is less certain. The walls are moist and the air humid, and
some species of moss and fungi thrive on them despite the darkness.
name: Dismal Cavern

desc:
The many large and sharp rocks jutting out from the steep mountainside dominate
the land here. Between the larger boulders, layers of scree have accumulated
into mounds of loose stony debris. Almost any movement causes debris to become
dislodged and tumble down the steep, rugged slope to the west. While to the
north the cliff is so steep that it prevents any travelling, the terrain looks
more easy to the east.
name: Jagged Mountains

desc:
There are signs of habitation here with bones and broken weapons accumulated
throughout the area on the ground. The remains of campfires can also be seen in
various locations but appear to be somewhat centred around one rocky area. The
central area has been fortified, but the years of disuse are starting to show on
the condition of the wooden walls and spikes. A huge cliff dominates the skyline
to the north and casts a shadow over this whole area.
name: Old Camp in the Mountains

desc:
The ground slopes sharply up to a dark pinnacle in the mountains. Movement here
will require travellers to scramble around on the loose rocks that carpet the
area, consuming a great deal of energy in the process. A few hardy fir trees
have managed to take root in the hard ground in small thickets as if huddled
together for protection. The slope to the south ends abruptly in a near-vertical
cliff.
name: Steep Slope in the Dark Mountains

desc:
The trees here are a combination of birch and beech; their trunks are twisted
and bent as if a great weight rests on them. The silence, along with the air
here, seems stifling. The trees seem to be encouraging the silence as not a
creak or groan from the suffering trees break it. The forest gets denser to the
east and seems to thin slightly as it approaches the mountains to the north.
name: Twisted Forest

desc:
A strange species of bracken grows amid the trees here. The younger, weaker
trees have been completely engulfed by the thickly knotted weed, making them
bend under the weight of the parasitic infestation. The bracken has an unnatural
yellow and green tint which adds an unusual splash of colour into this otherwise
dark forest.
name: Entwined Forest

desc:
The forest is completely untouched here, and every tree looks old, but healthy.
Gigantic black roots cover the ground and, unless they are very nimble, larger
animals may stumble here. Numerous small animals can be seen darting between
burrows made under the massive roots. It seems as if these small animals thrive
in the dark environment.
name: Ancient Forest

desc:
The branches of the trees surrounding the clearing seem to shift and move of
their own accord making it extremely difficult to see very far into the trees
bordering the glade. Only one species of dark oak grows here and each tree is
almost identical to its neighbour. This, together with the constant motion,
makes it very hard to navigate accurately.
name: Clearing in a Dark Forest

desc:
This place is the site of an ancient battle. The skeletal remains of the elven
and orkish participants can still be seen around the area where they fell in the
battle. It appears as though the forest vegetation is reluctant, even after all
these years, to regrow on the battle ground. A few petrified trunks, the
remnants of oaks long dead, stand vigil over the battlefield.
name: Old Battleground in the Forest

desc:
You have fallen down to the bottom of what once was an animal trap. At this
time, however, you are mostly amazed that your brain is still processing this
information, considering the fact that you have been impaled by several of the
stakes. You try to cry out, but the only thing that comes out of your mouth is
foaming blood and a quiet gargle.
name: Lethal Stakes

desc:
The area is dominated by a giant cobweb barricade that has been spun between the
trees to the south. The substantial, sticky mass must have been created over
many years as it is as solid and impenetrable as any wall. A few scattered bones
of an unidentified animal lie in the undergrowth stripped clean of all meat. To
the north and west, massive roots have been severed along the edge of the road
that runs near here. To the east, the ground begins to slope downward and
south-east, away from the road to the north.
name: Abysmal Depths of Mirkwood

desc:
The old road cuts straight through the forest on its way from the west to the
east. While the road is in a state of neglect, it is still possible to travel
through Mirkwood via the road, something that does not hold true for the
surrounding forest; dark and twisted trees lean on each other, their branches
intertwining together, blocking the passageways between most of the trees.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
The old road cuts straight through the forest on its way from the west to the
east. While the road is in a state of neglect, it is still possible to travel
through Mirkwood via the road, something that does not hold true for the
surrounding forest; dark and twisted trees lean on each other, their branches
intertwining together, blocking the passageways between most of the trees.
name: Old Forest Road
